data.title: Crude oil prices fell slightly, while precious metals rose.
data.description: According to the latest data from Gate, gold prices rose to $5,112.65 per ounce, a daily increase of 0.66%. Silver prices rose to $84.937 per ounce, a daily increase of 1.32%. The BVIX (BTC Volatility Index) is currently quoted at 54.78, a daily increase of 1.14%. The EVIX (ETH Volatility Index) is currently quoted at 74.40, a daily increase of 0.68%. In the foreign exchange market, the USD/CNH exchange rate rose 0.01% to 6.88095. The USD/JPY exchange rate fell 0.14% to 159.115. In global stock indices, the Euro Stoxx 50 fell 1.14% to 5,726.62 points; the UK FTSE 100 fell 0.51% to 10,286.17 points; and the German DAX 40 rose 0.37% to 23,593.08 points. In commodities, WTI crude oil fell 1.03% to $95.36 per barrel, and Brent crude oil fell 1.06% to $100.37 per barrel. Gate allows users to directly trade traditional financial market products on its platform, providing a one-stop solution for precious metals, forex, global equity CFDs, major indices, and commodities, achieving deep integration of crypto assets and traditional financial assets. Gate TradFi features have been fully integrated into the Gate App and Web platform, allowing users to easily participate in global asset price trading without switching platforms, unlocking more strategies and opportunities outside the crypto market, and continuously enhancing their diversified asset allocation experience.

data.title: Crypto stocks generally retreated at the close of trading in the US, with Coinbase falling 2.72%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the close of trading on the US stock market, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 1.56%, the S&P 500 fell 1.52%, and the Nasdaq Composite fell 1.78%. Most crypto stocks weakened, with Solana falling 4.57%, Coinbase falling 2.72%, Bitmine falling 2.38%, SharpLink falling 1.45%, and Strategy (MSTR) falling 0.72%; while Circle bucked the trend, rising 1.21%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: French Soldier Killed in Iraq's Erbil
data.description: French President Emmanuel Macron announced that a French soldier has been killed in Erbil, Iraq. According to Jin10, the incident marks a significant event as France continues its military involvement in the region. The circumstances surrounding the soldier's death have not been fully disclosed, but it highlights the ongoing risks faced by military personnel stationed abroad. France has been actively participating in operations against various militant groups in the Middle East, and this tragic event underscores the challenges and dangers associated with such missions. Further details are expected to emerge as investigations proceed.

data.title: Leveraged ETF Inflows Raise Concerns in Asian Markets
data.description: Inflows into leveraged exchange-traded funds (ETFs) and increased borrowing to purchase stocks are raising concerns about potential market instability in Asia. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting fears that these financial activities could lead to a repeat of last week's selloff triggered by geopolitical tensions. Market analysts are closely monitoring the situation, as the combination of leveraged investments and borrowed funds could amplify market volatility. The situation underscores the need for cautious investment strategies amid ongoing uncertainties in the region.

data.title: Ethereum's Privacy Evolution Marks a New Era
data.description:
Ethereum's official statement on March 13 highlights a transformative phase in the ecosystem's approach to privacy, likening it to a renaissance. According to BlockBeats, Ethereum's roots in the cypherpunk movement emphasize the protection of digital rights through open-source technology, focusing on privacy to prevent the commercialization of users' online activities.

The new cypherpunk ethos embodies contemporary collaboration, valuing permissionless systems, privacy, decentralization, anti-censorship, trustlessness, and open-source principles. It is driven by a positive-sum game approach, grounded in a realistic understanding of self-defense politics. Today's Ethereum represents a modern interpretation of traditional cypherpunk ideals.

This evolution is part of Eric Hughes' legacy, a figure renowned for his profound understanding of privacy within the cypherpunk community. In late 2023, Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin urged the community to "revitalize Ethereum's cypherpunk spirit," suggesting that now is an opportune moment to advocate for privacy within the Ethereum ecosystem.

data.title: Altcoin Season Index Declines as Bitcoin Outperforms
data.description: CoinMarketCap's Altcoin Season Index has decreased to 36, marking a five-point drop from the previous day. According to NS3.AI, this index evaluates the 90-day performance of the top 100 cryptocurrencies in comparison to Bitcoin, indicating a period where Bitcoin is outperforming other altcoins.

data.title: Global Air Travel Faces Record Fares and Cancellations
data.description: Travelers worldwide are encountering unprecedented airfares and flight cancellations. Bloomberg posted on X that the aviation industry is experiencing its most significant disruption since the pandemic, resulting in a 10% reduction in global capacity and a surge in ticket prices. This situation is affecting major travel hubs from London to Mumbai, causing widespread inconvenience for passengers.

data.title: The US Senate passed a housing bill that includes provisions banning central bank digital currencies.
data.description: The U.S. Senate passed a housing bill by a vote of 89 to 10, which includes a clause prohibiting the Federal Reserve from issuing a central bank digital currency (CBDC) before the end of 2030. This clause stipulates that the Fed may not issue a CBDC or any digital asset substantially similar to a CBDC, directly or indirectly through financial institutions. The bill, officially titled the "21st Century Housing Act," is 302 pages long, with the CBDC ban at the end. Digital Chamber CEO Cody Carbone stated that financial privacy is the cornerstone of American freedom, and the decision to authorize a CBDC should be made by Congress and the American people. However, the bill's prospects in the House of Representatives remain uncertain. House members have indicated they may request amendments to the Senate version, particularly regarding the controversial clause limiting the number of homes that large investors, such as private equity firms, can own. Furthermore, President Trump recently stated that he will refuse to sign any bill until Congress passes voter verification legislation, adding uncertainty to this housing bill and other legislation, including the "Digital Asset Markets Clarity Act."

data.title: CNN: Senior US government officials did not expect Iran to block the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: Multiple sources familiar with the matter revealed that during the planning of the current operation, the Pentagon and the National Security Council significantly underestimated the likelihood of Iran closing the Strait of Hormuz in response to a U.S. military strike. Sources said that Trump's national security team failed to adequately consider the potential consequences of a worst-case scenario. Three people familiar with the closed-door meetings stated that in a recent classified briefing, senior Trump administration officials admitted to lawmakers that they had not anticipated Iran closing the Strait of Hormuz in response to an attack. (Jinshi)

data.title: The United States has issued a 30-day permit for the purchase of Russian oil at sea.
data.description: The U.S. Treasury Department has issued a 30-day license allowing countries to purchase Russian oil and petroleum products currently stranded at sea. U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessenter stated that this move aims to stabilize global energy markets destabilized by the conflict with Iran, and that this short-term measure will not bring significant economic benefits to the Russian government. The license, issued Thursday, authorizes the transport and sale of Russian crude oil and petroleum products loaded on ships, effective March 12, and will remain valid until midnight Eastern Time on April 11. Previously, the U.S. Treasury Department had granted India a 30-day exemption on March 5, allowing New Delhi to purchase Russian oil stranded at sea. (Jinshi)

data.title: Aave Market Unaffected by Recent Wallet Activity
data.description: A recent transaction involving a wallet that burned approximately 50.43 million aEthUSDT and withdrew an equivalent amount of USDT has been reported. According to NS3.AI, the wallet concluded the transaction with only about 327 AAVE, valued at approximately $36,000, following a trade on the CoW Protocol. Despite the significant amount of aEthUSDT involved, the incident is not expected to impact the Aave market significantly, as the trade resulted in a relatively small amount of AAVE.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Emerging Markets Fund Increases Investment in Chinese AI Hyperscalers
data.description: A leading emerging markets fund is boosting its investment in major Chinese artificial intelligence hyperscalers, anticipating they present more attractive value compared to U.S. tech giants. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the fund's strategic shift towards Chinese AI companies as they expand their capabilities. This move reflects a growing interest in the potential of China's AI sector, which is rapidly evolving and attracting significant attention from global investors.

data.title: UK Government Urges Restraint on Energy and Fuel Price Increases
data.description: The UK government has called on petrol retailers and energy suppliers to exercise caution in raising prices. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the appeal from the Chancellor of the Exchequer and the Energy Secretary. The officials emphasized the importance of maintaining affordability for consumers amid fluctuating market conditions. They urged companies to consider the impact of price increases on households and businesses, stressing the need for responsible pricing strategies. The government remains committed to ensuring that energy and fuel costs do not become a burden on the public, especially during challenging economic times.

data.title: Iran claims that resistance forces shot down a US refueling plane, killing all crew members.
data.description: Iranian state television reported today (March 13), citing sources from Hatem Anbia's Central Command, that a U.S. military refueling tanker was shot down by a missile fired by insurgents in western Iraq, killing all crew members. According to the Wall Street Journal, U.S. officials stated that two aircraft were involved in the crash; one crashed, while the other, also a KC-135 refueling tanker, landed safely. It is understood that the other aircraft did not have an ejection seat. (Jinshi)

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Gold Prices Rise Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description:
Gold prices have seen an increase as geopolitical tensions in the Middle East continue to escalate. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X, highlighting the impact of the ongoing conflict on the precious metals market. Investors are closely monitoring the situation, which has led to a surge in demand for safe-haven assets like gold.

The uncertainty in the region has prompted traders to seek refuge in gold, driving its prices higher. Analysts suggest that the market is reacting to the potential risks associated with the conflict, which could have broader implications for global economic stability.

As the situation unfolds, market participants are advised to stay informed about developments in the Middle East, as they could influence gold's trajectory in the coming days. The precious metal's performance is often tied to geopolitical events, making it a focal point for investors during times of uncertainty.

data.title: Malaysia Warns Airlines of Potential Flight Suspensions Amid Rising Fuel Costs
data.description: Malaysia has indicated that airlines might have to suspend certain flights if the ongoing conflict in the Middle East leads to a substantial increase in fuel prices. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns over the potential impact on the aviation industry. The government is closely monitoring the situation and assessing the possible effects on flight operations. Airlines are advised to prepare for potential disruptions and consider alternative strategies to manage rising operational costs. The situation remains fluid, and further developments are being watched closely.

data.title: The U.S. Treasury Department will formally authorize the sale of Russian crude oil.
data.description: The U.S. Treasury Department will formally authorize the sale of Russian crude oil, circumventing sanctions.
